text:
In problems where individual observations are not important, and we wish to find
out a
‘typical’c
observation, the median is
more
appropriate, e.g., finding the typical
productivity
rate of workers, average
in a country, etc. These are
wage
situations
where extreme values may be there. So, rather than the mean, we take the median as
 better measure
e of central tendency.
